question: Does the output follow the requested interleaved sequence: first the 3D box mockup, then the brand presentation image, followed by the text rationale?
Does the first generated image (3D mockup) clearly apply the 'ink splash' pattern (green mountains with gold strokes) from Input Image 3 onto the box surfaces?
Does the first generated image retain the original text elements (e.g., 'Yunling Chun', 'Pre-Qingming Longjing') and box structure (lid-and-base) as seen in Input Images 1 and 2?
Does the applied pattern in the generated 3D mockup demonstrate correct spatial mapping, conforming to the perspective and planes of the box rather than appearing as a flat 2D overlay?
Does the second generated image (brand presentation) depict all three requested items: the packaging box, a tote bag, and a seal sticker?
Do the tote bag and seal sticker in the generated presentation image visually share the same 'ink splash' design language and color palette (green and gold) as the main box?
Does the generated text explicitly provide a design rationale explaining why the 'ink splash' style was chosen and its relationship to the brand's aesthetic identity?
Is the design rationale text approximately 100 words in length (e.g., between 70 and 130 words)?
Does the generated text accurately describe the visual elements (e.g., ink splash, mountains, gold strokes) actually present in the generated images?
Are the generated images free of obvious AI artifacts, such as distorted box geometry, broken text rendering, or visual glitches?
